meta.title: Health & Safety File — CDM 2015 Reg 12(5)–(9)
meta.subtitle: As-built residual-risk record handed to the client — Tower B fit-out
meta.author: Principal Designer (prepares & maintains under Reg 12(5))
meta.classification: Restricted distribution — client + future-work duty-holders; competent-person review required
meta.deid_notice: Individuals named in commissioning, survey, and as-built records are referred to by role label; no worker health detail is carried into this file. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This Health & Safety File is prepared and maintained by the principal designer under CDM 2015 Regulation 12(5) and handed to the client at completion (Reg 12(6)-(9)). It records, for the Tower B office fit-out (levels 3-9, occupied commercial use), the residual and unusual hazards a future construction, maintenance, cleaning, refurbishment, or demolition worker could NOT reasonably anticipate from the structure itself — it is NOT a copy of the general specification.

items: Key as-built drawings held: architectural AB-07, services AB-04, structural SB-02 (rev C).

Services & isolation points: LV distribution board in the level-9 plant room; the AHU isolation point is non-standard — located in the level-9 riser, not at the unit (a future worker could not anticipate this).
[GAP] No commissioning record held for the level-9 AHU — owner + date in the information-gaps register below.

rows: Glazing is non-load-bearing and visually indistinguishable from walk-on glazing — a cleaner could not anticipate the fragility
No permanent edge protection at high-level plant
Edge protection was removed at handover; the deck looks finished but has no guarding
Single restricted egress + possible atmosphere change on shutdown — not evident from the door
Buried services on an unusual route
Incoming LV runs diagonally across the courtyard, not along the boundary as a worker would assume
sections.caption: Residual-only discipline: each hazard is one a competent future worker could NOT reasonably anticipate, and is LOCATED. A routine/anticipatable hazard or a general-spec dump is excluded.

rows.title: Presumed asbestos in riser lagging
rows.description: Duct lagging in risers 3 and 5 is presumed to contain asbestos pending a refurbishment-and-demolition survey; no riser disturbance until surveyed and a management plan is in force (hold-point).
rows.evidence: Asbestos register rev B + gap register G-1

sections.text: Maintenance / cleaning / refurb / demolition safety arrangements

rows.control: Specify a permanent edge-protection + anchor system for the atrium-roof cleaning route at next refurbishment (designed-in, before any harness-only access)
Fragile-roof safe-system-of-work + crawl boards / staging for any atrium-glazing access; never walk on the glazing
Asbestos management plan + R&D survey before any riser disturbance (refurb/demolition)
Permit-to-work + confined-space entry procedure for the B2 plant room

rows.action: G-1 — Commission a refurbishment-and-demolition asbestos survey of risers 3 and 5 before any disturbance
G-2 — Obtain / reconstruct the level-9 AHU commissioning record (isolation sequence)

sections.text: Residual-only discipline: only hazards a future worker could NOT reasonably anticipate are recorded, each located. This file is NOT a general-spec dump. No work on riser lagging may proceed until the asbestos survey (G-1) is complete and the management plan is in force.

Rev 1.0 — completion handover. This file is a living record: any update or single-package addition APPENDS under a new revision and NEVER overwrites prior residual-risk content. The principal designer maintains it through the project (Reg 12(5)); at completion it is handed to the client, who retains it and makes it available for future work (Reg 12(6)-(9)).
This Health & Safety File completes the chain PCI -> CPP -> H&S File (CDM 2015 Reg 4 -> 12 -> 12(5)). This file does not assume the PCI or CPP skill has run (loose coupling).

items: CDM 2015 (SI 2015/51) Regulation 12(5) — the principal designer prepares & maintains the Health & Safety File
CDM 2015 Regulation 12(6)-(9) — review, update, handover to the client at completion, client retention/availability
HSE L153 ACOP — Appendix 4 H&S-file content list + the could-not-reasonably-anticipate residual-hazard test
ISO 45001 §6.1.2 / §7.5 / §8.1.2 — hazard identification, documented information, hierarchy of controls
Control of Asbestos Regulations — duty to manage asbestos in situ
India: BOCW completion records via hse-india / bocw-compliance — state resolved first, form reference [GAP], never a national form number

sections.text: Decision-support only. This Health & Safety File must be reviewed by a competent person before issue. SME review (CDM Principal Designer persona) ran as decision-support; it does not constitute competent-person approval.
